items.title: Document hierarchy
items.scenario: A Markdown spec parses into Title → Sections → Sub-sections → Paragraphs.
items.explanation: Trees give natural parent-child structure for slicing context (e.g. 'embed each section as one chunk').

items.title: Tree-of-thought prompting
items.scenario: An LLM explores multiple intermediate reasoning steps and picks the best branch.
items.explanation: Each thought is a node; siblings are alternative continuations. A tree lets you backtrack and score branches.

items.title: BPE merge tree
items.scenario: A byte-pair encoder learns merges 't h' → 'th', 'th e' → 'the', etc.
items.explanation: Merges form a tree: leaves are raw bytes, internal nodes are merged token pieces.

items.title: Conversation forks
items.scenario: An agent retries with a different system prompt and creates a branched history.
items.explanation: Conversation state turns into a tree of variants; a BST/sorted index can keep them ordered by timestamp.
